title: Anthropic upgrades Claude Opus 4.8 for coding, agents and long-running work
friendlyTitle: Claude gets better at coding and longer tasks
summary: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.8, an Opus-class upgrade focused on coding, agentic reliability, reasoning, professional workflows and more efficient fast mode. The launch also adds Claude Code dynamic workflows and effort controls in Claude.ai and Cowork.
friendlySummary: Anthropic updated Claude Opus with stronger coding, reasoning and agent skills. It is meant to work more reliably on bigger jobs.
whyItMatters: For people using AI as a daily collaborator, the important shift is not just benchmark lift but fewer tool-calling mistakes, better self-checking and more dependable long-running work. Teams building with Claude get a stronger default model without changing the basic workflow.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This can make Claude more useful for everyday work where you need an AI helper to keep track of a task and avoid simple mistakes.
